title: parse_proxy_addr: credentials in URL silently ignored, no SOCKS5 auth
description:
If a user passes socks5://user:pass@host:port, parse_proxy_addr uses rsplit_once(':') which correctly extracts the port, but the userinfo (user:pass@) is folded into the host string passed to Socks5Stream::connect(). tokio-socks never sees the credentials as SOCKS5 username/password auth (RFC 1929) — it just tries to connect to the literal string 'user:pass@host', which fails with an opaque TCP connection error.

Two distinct problems:
1. Credentials are not used for SOCKS5 username/password authentication (RFC 1929). A proxy that requires auth silently fails with a misleading error.
2. No input validation rejects or warns about user:pass@ in the URL, so users who include credentials receive no feedback that they are being silently dropped.

The connection error message will say 'SOCKS5 tunnel connect failed' with a TCP-level error, giving no indication that the proxy rejected authentication.

File: core/src/transport.rs, function parse_proxy_addr (~line 423).
The existing test proxy_addr_parses_port_ignores_credentials (line 562) explicitly documents this behavior as known but does not flag it as a bug.
